title: The Foxglove King
author: Hannah Whitten
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "The Foxglove King" book by Hannah Whitten?
Options:
A. I had succeeded once before. You are unable to accomplish this now, Anton said gently. There are hundreds of dead bodies. Lore, even for someone as skilled as you, channeling that much death magic would be nearly impossible. You must let me try! She struggled not to sob there, not with them watching, but she was so angry and overwhelmed and it was always most difficult to restrain her tears when overwhelmed, thinking of the catacombs beneath, full of howling corpses that had been living people, ordinary people. So this explains why you led us here. Bastian's voice, calm and cold and penetrating her panic. His gaze was locked on Gabe.  
B. I did once before. You cant this time, Anton said gently. Its hundreds of bodies. Lore even for you, channeling that much Mortem would be nearly impossible. You have to let me try! She didnt want to cry here, not in front of them, but she was so angry and overwhelmed and crying was always hardest to fight off when she was overwhelmed, thinking of the catacombs beneath them, full of screaming corpses whod been people, just people So this is why you led us down there. Bastians voice, calm and cold and cutting through her panic. His gaze was squared on Gabe.
C. I had managed it once previously. You can't do it this time, Anton said kindly. There are hundreds of corpses. Lore, even for you, summoning that much death power would be nearly inconceivable. You need to let me attempt this! She didn't want to weep here, not before them, but she was so furious and overwhelmed and weeping was always most challenging to resist when overwhelmed, thinking of the catacombs below, full of wailing bodies that had been living people, regular people. So this is why you took us down here. Bastian's voice, composed and icy and cutting through her panic. His stare was fixed firmly on Gabe.
D. I had done this once in the past. You can't do it now, Anton said kindly. It's hundreds of corpses. Lore, even for you, summoning that much death energy would be nearly unachievable. You need to allow me to attempt it! She didn't want to weep here, not in their presence, but she was so furious and overpowered and crying was always most difficult to resist when she was overpowered, thinking of the catacombs below them, full of shrieking bodies that had been people, just regular people. So this is the reason you guided us down there. Bastian's voice, composed and icy and cutting through her panic. His stare was fixed on Gabe.
Answer:
